Table rules: Use Aspose.Words.Drawing.Shape with ShapeType.WordArt to create a text watermark inside a table cell.
Do not use TextWatermarkOptions for cell‑level watermarks; they apply to the whole document.
Insert the Shape into the target Cell's paragraph (e.g., cell.FirstParagraph.AppendChild).
Do not use RelativeVerticalPosition.Row because the enum does not contain this member in the referenced Aspose.Words version.
Add HorizontalAlignment and VerticalAlignment to centre the watermark inside the cell.
Saving/Export rules: Add the Aspose.Words.Saving namespace when using save options.
Use the Document constructor that accepts a file path to load an HTML document (do not call a non‑existent Load method).
Save the modified document with Document.Save, specifying the desired output file name and format.
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Set TextPath properties (Text, FontFamily, FontSize, Bold) on the Shape.
Remove the unnecessary Aspose.Words.Drawing namespace import that caused the TextPath/FontSize conflict.
Remove the non‑existent TextPath.FontSize property; control text size via the shape's Height/Width.
Keep the required using directives and ensure the shape is inserted with InsertNode.
Layout rules: Adjust rotation (e.g., -45°) to achieve a diagonal layout similar to WatermarkLayout.Diagonal.
Watermark rules: Access the watermark via the Document.Watermark property and call SetText (or SetText with TextWatermarkOptions) to add a text watermark.
